hero.question: Will Michigan win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take:
34.5% prices Michigan as a top-8 contender nationally, but that's generous for a program that hasn't won a title since 1989 and just lost its best player to the portal.

The real tell is volume stayed flat while the market sits at a 5-year high for Wolverines tournament odds, suggesting sharp money isn't buying the hype despite everyone else piling in.

daily_take.headline: Michigan's Tournament Odds Stuck at 34.5%. The Smart Money Knows Why.
daily_take.deck: Michigan's NCAA title odds haven't budged in 24 hours while the broader betting public rotates into Arizona and other challengers. The market is pricing in brutal Big Ten competition and questions about whether this roster can survive March, not hype. Sharp bettors should ask whether 34.5% reflects real weakness or undervalues a blue-chip program with recruiting momentum heading into next season.

sidebar.headline: Arizona jumped 2.2 points while Michigan flatlined. The market is hedging toward multiple contenders instead of coronating one favorite.
